title: Punk Rocker Birds Win a Big Photo Contest!
summary:
Have you ever seen a bird with a mohawk? Well, three baby egrets (a type of white bird) have feathers that stick up just like punk rockers' hair! That's why their photo is called "The Punk Rockers." It won the grand prize in Smithsonian Magazine's 23rd annual photography contest.

The photographer, Jacqueline Burke, is a 64-year-old veterinarian (an animal doctor). She took the picture at a rookery (a place where birds raise their babies) in New Jersey. The chicks were no bigger than golf balls when curled up! "They were squabbling nonstop," she said. One would peck, and the other would peck back.

Over 17,000 photos from 108 countries were entered. Other winners include a woman dancing barefoot at a Hindu festival, a teenager on a horse during a Navajo ceremony, a colorful chameleon, and a child cleaning a bull's horns in South Sudan.

Burke used to watch birds with binoculars. In 2014, she switched to a camera. "We need to save species like this and save moments like this," she said.
why_it_matters: It shows that even small moments in nature can be celebrated and that taking photos can help protect animals.

keywords.explanation: The name of the winning photo, referring to the birds' feather mohawks.
Young white birds whose feathers stick up like punk rockers' hair.
The top award in the contest, which the punk rocker birds photo won.
An animal doctor; the photographer Jacqueline Burke is a veterinarian.
A place where birds raise their babies; Burke took the photo at a rookery in New Jersey.

questions.question: What is the title of the winning photo?
How many photos were entered into the contest?
What type of bird are the chicks in the photo?
Where was the rookery where the photo was taken?
When did Jacqueline Burke switch from binoculars to a camera?

background_read: The Smithsonian Institution is a famous group of museums and research centers in the United States.
Its magazine holds a photography contest every year to celebrate amazing photos from around the world.
Many people love taking pictures of birds because they are beautiful and interesting.
Article_Structure: WHO: Jacqueline Burke, a veterinarian, took the photo.
WHAT: The photo called 'The Punk Rockers' won the grand prize in Smithsonian Magazine's contest.
WHERE: The photo was taken at a rookery in New Jersey.
WHY: The birds' mohawk-like feathers made the photo stand out, and Burke wanted to raise awareness about protecting wildlife.

perspectives.description: The contest gives amateur photographers a chance to be recognized and share amazing moments.
The photo highlights how nature can be entertaining and artistic.
Some might say the contest focuses too much on cute or dramatic images rather than important news.
